REFERENCE: This reference contains multiple critical inconsistencies: (1) DOI prefix matches NEJM, not Nature; (2) authors don't match the article associated with the DOI; (3) title appears fabricated or nonsensical; (4) volume number (7772) is implausible for Nature; (5) PMCID format doesn't match the 2019 date. The reference appears to be fabricated or contains errors so severe as to make it invalid.
authors: The author list 'Hondele M, Sachdev R, Heinrich S, Wang J, Vallotton P, Fontoura BMA, Weis K' does not match the typical author list of the cited NEJM article on tofacitinib in COVID-19 pneumonia (Stone et al.), nor does it match a Nature article on microbial membrane proteins. The combination of authors and journal/doi mismatch is suspicious.
title: The title 'From microbial membrane proteins to the mysteries of emotion' appears to be a nonsensical or fabricated title, not a real article title. It doesn't correspond to any known publication.
journal_name: The journal is listed as 'Nature', but the DOI prefix '10.1136/NEJMoa2116044' belongs to the New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM), not Nature. This is a clear mismatch.
journal_volume: Volume and page information '(7772):144-148' is given. Nature volume 7772 with pages 144-148 does not correspond to a real Nature volume (Nature's current volumes are in the 600s range). Volume 7772 is implausible.
journal_issue: No specific issue number is given beyond the volume. Cannot validate.
journal_page: Pages '144-148' are provided but cannot be verified against the stated journal or volume, which appear fabricated.
publication_date: The publication date '2019 Sep' is plausible but cannot be verified given the multiple other inconsistencies.
doi: The DOI '10.1136/NEJMoa2116044' belongs to NEJM (publisher prefix 10.1136 is BMJ/NEJM), not to Nature (which uses 10.1038). The DOI itself corresponds to Stone et al. 2020 in NEJM, which doesn't match the stated authors, title, or date.
pmcid: PMCID 'PMC12124552' - PMC IDs typically have a specific format. PMC12124552 would correspond to a very recent article (PMCs in the 12 million range are from 2024-2025), which doesn't match the 2019 publication date. This is suspicious.
pmid: PMID 31435012 cannot be verified with certainty, but given the multiple other inconsistencies (authors, title, journal, DOI, volume), this PMID is likely fabricated or misattributed.
